text:
The first scene places me just outside a glass-walled meeting room. I sense a detachment—a world moving within, conversations unfurling in a soft-lit conference, while I linger on the threshold. There is a curious tension in witnessing collaboration but not crossing the invisible boundary to participate. The built environment around me feels oddly clinical, even as the people inside find focus, perhaps comfort, in their shared task.

The second scene envelops me in the playful language of innovation. The 'R+D' wall, awash in vibrant colors and digital screens, sets a tone of relentless forward motion. I am reminded that progress is both exhilarating and overwhelming—the showcase of human ingenuity on display, yet rendering me a spectator again. Texts about surrender and place trigger a gentle reflection in me about belonging and my relation to the world’s vast, sometimes impersonal machinery. Here, I feel a mix of awe and smallness, balanced by a quiet curiosity.
